task_pressure: A fresh agent session needs one operational eval front door that shows tools, active routes, freshness blockers, candidate packets, and stop-lines.
actual_trajectory: The existing session-start packet exposed first commands and a candidate summary, but not the real packet routes needed for immediate work.
first_failure: The front door stopped at summary-level candidate queue data and still carried a synthetic deferred session-mining item.
consequence: Future agents would rediscover tools, queue state, and manual-mining context instead of starting from actionable current routes.
privacy_boundary: The packet records only the operational route gap and bounded refs; no private transcript body is copied.
existing_surface_check: The dashboard/session-start scripts exist and need packet route projection; no proof bundle or central adoption is claimed.
candidate_state_reason: The operational gap is concrete and directly tied to the per-session eval front door, but owner review decides whether it becomes a durable eval case.
promotion_forbidden_until: session-start packet exposes real packet routes
dashboard shape validation rejects synthetic completion overclaim
candidate converted into runtime smoke or trajectory eval by owner
next_step: Import real packets into the dashboard queue and expose top candidate routes from session-start output.
